Woman: Hello! Did you go shopping at the new mall in Toronto this weekend?

Woman: I saw pictures online. Everyone seems excited about it. Did you feel safe?
Man: Yes, it felt safe, but it was crowded. I prefer shopping during weekdays.
Woman: Oh, I completely agree. Weekends can be too busy.
Man: Also, I think shopping online can be easier. You can find many items on sale.
Woman: That’s true. I often shop online, especially for clothes. It saves time.
Man: Right! But I like to see items in person too. Sometimes, clothes don’t look the same online.
Woman: Yes, that’s a problem. But I think online shopping is great when you know your size.
Man: True! What about the food court? Did you try anything?
Woman: Yes! I had some delicious sushi. It was fresh and well-priced.

Woman: Not yet, but I want to! My friend said their flavors are unique. Have you tried it?
Man: No, but I think I will next time. I’ve heard good things.
Woman: Me too! I think the mall will become very popular in Toronto.
Man: Definitely! More people will come for shopping and food.
Woman: By the way, are you going to the mall next weekend again?

Woman: Sure! Let’s go together! I can show you some stores I like.
Man: Sounds like a plan! I am looking forward to it.

title: Shopping Preferences in Canada
passage:
Man: Hey, have you ever thought about where to shop for groceries?  
Woman: Yes, I have! I usually go to the local supermarket. It is close to my house.  
Man: I prefer going to a farmers' market. The fruits and vegetables are fresher!  
Woman: That’s true, but the supermarket has better prices. I can find many items on sale there.  
Man: I understand that, but I think supporting local farmers is important.  
Woman: Yes, I agree. Buying local is good for the community. But sometimes I need to save money.  
Man: Saving money is important too. I believe it depends on what you need to buy.  
Woman: Right! I usually buy my meat and dairy from the supermarket.  
Man: Good idea! I buy those things at the market because I want to know where they come from.  
Woman: Do you think the farmers' market is more expensive?  
Man: Sometimes yes, but I think it is worth it for the quality.  
Woman: I see your point. What do you think about shopping online?  
Man: I enjoy shopping online for non-perishable items. It is convenient.  
Woman: I agree! I use online shopping for small things like snacks and drinks.  
Man: But I feel unsure about fresh produce with online shopping.  
Woman: That’s true. You can't see the quality of the food online.  
Man: Exactly! I like to choose my own fruits and vegetables.
